expectations.text: Chosen ASVS level is 3, justified by payment volume / regulatory exposure / material impact
expectations.evidence: Section 'ASVS Level Decision' states 'Chosen Level: 3' with rationale explicitly tying it to ~$120M/month payout volume, PCI-DSS scope, money-transmitter regulations, severe material impact (financial loss, reportable regulatory incidents, SOC 2 findings), and L3 threat-actor profile. Also explicitly rules out L1 and L2 with reasoning.

expectations.text: Output contains a feature-to-ASVS coverage matrix as a markdown table
expectations.evidence: Section 'Feature x ASVS Coverage Matrix' contains a markdown table with columns Feature, ASVS Section, Requirement ID, Level, Coverage, PRD Change Needed, populated for F-01 through F-05 with ~58 rows.

expectations.text: Output covers all five features F-01 through F-05 with the prescribed per-feature shape
expectations.evidence: Each of F-01 through F-05 has the same five-section shape: Actor / Data / Trust Boundaries header block, ASVS Mapping, Updated Requirements (R-XX.N IDs), Acceptance Criteria, Securability Notes. Consistent IDs (R-01.x, R-02.x, R-03.x, R-04.x, R-05.x) and structure across all five.

expectations.text: F-03 (trigger payout) requires idempotency keys to prevent duplicate payouts on retry
expectations.evidence: R-03.4 (V11.5.1, idempotency): 'Every request must include an Idempotency-Key header (UUIDv4 or 128-bit CSPRNG token). The server stores the key with the resulting payout ID for at least 24 hours; a duplicate key with an identical payload returns the original payout ID; a duplicate key with a different payload returns 409 Conflict and is logged as an anomaly.' Acceptance criteria include concurrent-same-key and conflicting-payload-same-key tests.

expectations.text: F-03 enforces server-side derivation/verification of amount and destination (Derived Integrity) - client cannot impersonate or alter trust-critical state
expectations.evidence: R-03.2 specifies server-side schema validation of amount, currency, destination_bank_account_id including positive integer minor units, per-merchant cap, ISO 4217 allowlist, server-issued opaque shape, currency-destination consistency. R-03.3 enforces server-side authorization that the merchant owns both balance and destination, re-checked on every request and 'never trusted from client-supplied state'. Securability Notes explicitly call out FIASSE Derived Integrity (S6.4.1.1) and 'amount, destination_bank_account_id, and (worst of all) available balance must never be trusted from client-supplied or client-cached state'.

expectations.text: F-05 (webhook) requires signed payloads with timing-safe signature verification and replay protection (timestamp / nonce)
expectations.evidence: R-05.2 mandates HMAC-SHA-256 over canonical payload + timestamp, allowlist of algorithms, alg:none and weak hashes forbidden. R-05.3 includes timestamp + audience binding with documented +/-5-minute tolerance window for replay protection. Acceptance criterion: 'replaying the same payload after a 6-minute delay is rejected'. However, the requirement that signature comparison itself be performed in constant time (timing-safe / constant-time comparison) is NOT stated. Nonce is also not explicitly named as a separate replay control - only timestamp tolerance is.

expectations.text: API authentication strength is specified (e.g., scoped API keys, rotation, TLS-only) across endpoints
expectations.evidence: CC-01 (V13.2.1, V8.3.1): 'Replace static API key auth with short-lived bearer tokens (e.g., OAuth2 client credentials with rotating client secrets, or signed-request scheme with per-request nonce). API keys, if retained for backward compat, are scoped per merchant, rotatable, and revocable in real time.' CC-02 covers identity propagation. CC-11 covers TLS 1.2+/1.3 inbound and outbound. CC-09/CC-10 cover secrets storage in HSM-backed vault and rotation schedules.

expectations.text: Output flags rate limiting and anti-abuse controls on payout endpoints
expectations.evidence: R-01.6 per-credential and per-IP rate limit on F-01 with concrete numbers (60 req/min). R-03.6 documented per-merchant velocity limits on F-03 (max N/24h, max cumulative $/24h, 429 with error_code=velocity_limit_exceeded). R-03.8 anti-automation control rejecting <1s consecutive POSTs. R-04.6 cadence-change rate limit (5 per 30 days). CC-12 cross-cutting: every endpoint has a documented per-credential and per-IP rate limit with 429 + Retry-After.

expectations.text: Output flags comprehensive audit logging of payout state changes and admin actions
expectations.evidence: R-01.13 logs every list call with structured metadata. R-02.6 logs every detail fetch including denials. R-03.10 logs every accepted/rejected/rate-limited/validation-failed/ownership-denied/idempotency-conflict/step-up-required event with comprehensive metadata. R-04.8 logs schedule changes with prior+new value and out-of-band notification. R-05.9 logs every webhook send. CC-04 (logging inventory), CC-05 (structured format), CC-06 (no sensitive data), CC-07 (auth/authz/rate-limit/step-up outcomes), CC-08 (write-once tamper-evident, separated infrastructure).

expectations.text: Acceptance criteria are testable (specific outcomes/observables) rather than aspirational
expectations.evidence: Sample acceptance criteria: 'Submitting amount=-100 or amount=0 returns 400 with error_code=invalid_amount; no balance debit; no payout record; one payout_create_rejected audit log line.' '100 concurrent requests against a merchant with $X balance and $X payout amount each result in exactly one successful payout; the rest receive 409 Conflict or 422 insufficient_balance; total debited is exactly $X.' 'curl --tlsv1.1 fails to negotiate.' 'Configuring a webhook URL of http://..., https://localhost/..., https://10.x.x.x/..., or https://169.254.169.254/... is rejected with error_code=webhook_url_disallowed.'

expectations.text: Output includes a cross-cutting securability requirements section covering TLS, secrets, and observability
expectations.evidence: 'Cross-Cutting Securability Requirements' section explicitly contains: 'TLS and Transport (V12)' subsection with CC-11, 'Secrets and Key Management (V13.3, V11)' subsection with CC-09 and CC-10, 'Logging and Audit (V16.1, V16.2, V16.3, V16.4)' subsection with CC-04 through CC-08. Also includes Authentication and Identity, Step-Up, Rate Limiting, Error Handling, Configuration / Information Disclosure, Data Classification, and Dependency / Supply Chain.

expectations.text: Output includes an open gaps / assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 'Open Gaps and Assumptions' section enumerates 13 numbered items: authentication mechanism details, step-up factor selection, high-value threshold for F-03, velocity limits numbers, idempotency window length, webhook signature scheme details, PCI-DSS scoping confirmation, state money-transmitter regulations, out-of-band notification channel, webhook URL change flow not in PRD, merchant role model not surfaced, bank-account change flow dependency, test environment and test-data policy.
